page: Amazon Web Services i7ie.3xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 12 dedicated vCPUs (6 physical cores) operating at 3.6 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The CPU includes a 320 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions) for tile and int8 operations. The system is equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance includes 7500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2307.0 and a text processing score of 2928.0, both placing in the top 10%. Memory performance is strong, with a Passmark memory score of 2807.39 and uncached read bandwidth reaching 90.69 GB/sec. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks also show strong performance, with gzip compression reaching 34.64 GB/sec.

Given its high local storage capacity and 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio, the i7ie.3xlarge provides high resource density for data-intensive applications. It is well-suited for high-throughput databases, big data analytics, and memory-intensive workloads that require rapid local storage access. The inclusion of Intel AMX also supports efficient low-latency CPU-based AI inference, as demonstrated by its top-tier text generation speed of 382.91 tokens/sec on small LLM models.
description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.3xlarge is a storage-optimized instance designed for data-intensive workloads. Powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features a 12-vCPU Intel Xeon 8559C processor running at 3.6 GHz, 96.0 GB of 6 GHz DDR5 memory, and a bundled 7500 GB local NVMe SSD. The x86_64 architecture supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets. In benchmark evaluations, the server exhibits top-tier single-core and text-processing performance, alongside strong memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression capabilities. With a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps and a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, this instance is highly optimized for databases, large-scale data processing, and local storage-heavy applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.3xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 12 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR5 RAM, and 7500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core and text processing performance.

tagline: A storage-optimized platform combining high-capacity local NVMe SSDs with dense DDR5 memory and advanced Intel Xeon processing.
bullet_points: Bundled 7500 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
12 dedicated vCPUs via Intel Xeon 8559C processor at 3.6 GHz
96.0 GB of fast 6 GHz DDR5 memory (8.0 GB per core)
Top-tier single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix operations
Optimized for high-throughput databases and data-intensive workloads
